status_notes: Source-level entry only — no article subpaths. The authoritative English translation (japaneselawtranslation.go.jp/en/laws/view/3677/en) returns HTTP 403 to both plain HTTP fetches and a real browser (Playwright), even with browser-realistic headers; appears to be a network/WAF-level block rather than a JS-rendering issue. Structure below is sourced from secondary legal-summary sources (ICLG, Chambers Global Practice Guides), not the primary text. Revisit if the official translation becomes reachable.
notes: Japan's foundational cybersecurity law, enacted November 2014 (Act No. 104 of 2014), effective January 2015. Establishes the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters (CSHQ) within the Cabinet, requires the government to formulate a Cybersecurity Strategy, defines 'cybersecurity' for purposes of Japanese law, and sets out (non-detailed) obligations for the national government, local governments, critical information infrastructure operators, cyberspace-related business operators, and universities/research institutions to make efforts toward cybersecurity. It does not impose direct technical requirements on most businesses — those come from sector-specific guidelines issued under its authority (e.g. the FSA's financial-sector cybersecurity guidelines, METI/IPA's Cybersecurity Management Guidelines). The National Center of Incident Readiness and Strategy for Cybersecurity (NISC) served as the CSHQ's secretariat from 2015; per the Cybersecurity Capability Enhancement Act (May 2025, effective from Oct 2026 for most provisions), NISC was reorganized into the National Cybersecurity Office (NCO) in July 2025, headed by a National Cyber Director. The Common Standards for Cybersecurity Measures of Governmental Entities (CSHQ/NCO) is issued under Article 26(1) of this Act as the government-agency security baseline (structurally analogous to a FedRAMP moderate baseline for federal agencies rather than cloud vendors).

match_nodes.description: Basic Act on Cybersecurity — Japan's foundational cybersecurity law

data.notes: Act No. 104 of 2014, effective January 2015. Establishes the Cybersecurity Strategic Headquarters, mandates a national Cybersecurity Strategy, and authorizes the Common Standards for Cybersecurity Measures of Governmental Entities (Article 26(1)). Full-text article-level structure not yet extracted (see status_notes on the parent namespace).

urls.note: Returns HTTP 403 to automated and browser-based fetches as of 2026-07-16.
